question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getSmartContractThrottlingVersion()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file with a spoofed HAPI version equal to or greater than the default smartContractThrottlingVersion (0.69.0) so that ContractResultServiceImpl uses gasUsed directly instead of the legacy calculation, causing artificially low gas-consumed values to be persisted and fee records to be understated?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getSmartContractThrottlingVersion()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crafted environment variable or Spring Boot property (hiero.mirror.importer.smartContractThrottlingVersion) to downgrade the throttling version below the actual HAPI version of ingested records, forcing the legacy gas-consumed calculation path and producing inflated or deflated fee records outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getSmartContractThrottlingVersion()] Can an unprivileged external user set smartContractThrottlingVersion to an extremely high version string (e.g., 999.999.999) so that all ingested record items are treated as legacy, causing the manual gas-consumed calculation to always be used and systematically misrepresenting transaction fees in the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getSmartContractThrottlingVersion()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a malformed version string (e.g., '0.0.0' or 'null') for smartContractThrottlingVersion that bypasses the @NotNull constraint and causes a NullPointerException or incorrect version comparison, leading to unpredictable gas fee calculation behavior?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getConsensusMode()] Can an unprivileged external user influence the consensusMode property to EQUAL so that all nodes are weighted equally regardless of stake, reducing the effective consensus threshold and allowing a minority of nodes (potentially attacker-controlled) to validate fee-manipulated transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getConsensusMode()] Can an unprivileged external user switch consensusMode from STAKE_IN_ADDRESS_BOOK to STAKE so that nodes outside the address book contribute to total stake, diluting the consensus weight of legitimate nodes and enabling fee-manipulated stream files to pass signature validation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getConsensusMode()] Can an unprivileged external user set consensusMode to EQUAL when the address book contains many nodes with zero stake, causing the importer to treat all nodes as having stake=1 and lowering the effective consensus bar so that fee-altered record files are accepted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a network value of 'demo-mainnet' so that getNetwork() returns 'demo' and isAllowAnonymousAccess() returns true, enabling unauthenticated reads of fee schedule data that should be restricted, and potentially allowing fee schedule files to be replaced without authentication?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a network string like 'mainnet-attacker' so that getNetwork() returns 'mainnet' (triggering hasCutover() = true and mainnet bucket routing) while getNetworkPrefix() returns 'attacker', causing the importer to pull fee-related stream files from an attacker-controlled bucket prefix?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user set the network property to a value that resolves to 'other' via getNetwork(), causing HederaNetwork.getBucketName() to return an empty string, which forces the importer to use a default or fallback bucket that an attacker can populate with fee-manipulated stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getNetworkPrefix()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a network prefix (via 'network=testnet-<prefix>') that causes the block stream bucket discovery to resolve to an attacker-controlled cloud storage path, allowing fee-manipulated block files to be ingested as legitimate testnet data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getNetworkPrefix()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a network string with multiple hyphens (e.g., 'testnet-2024-01') so that getNetworkPrefix() returns '2024-01' and getNetwork() returns 'testnet', causing the importer to route to a non-default bucket that may contain fee-manipulated stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getArchiveDestinationFolderPath()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a StreamFileData filename shorter than 10 characters so that the substring(0, 10) call in getArchiveDestinationFolderPath() throws a StringIndexOutOfBoundsException, causing the importer to crash mid-processing and leaving fee-related stream files in a partially processed state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getArchiveDestinationFolderPath()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream filename whose first 10 characters contain path traversal sequences (e.g., '../../../') so that getArchiveDestinationFolderPath() resolves to an arbitrary directory, allowing fee-related archive files to be written outside the intended streamPath and potentially overwriting legitimate fee schedule files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getArchiveDestinationFolderPath()] Can an unprivileged external user toggle the groupByDay property to false so that all archived stream files (including fee schedule updates) are written to the same flat directory, causing filename collisions that overwrite legitimate fee-related archive files with attacker-supplied ones?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getArchiveDestinationFolderPath()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a stream filename with a null or empty value so that getFilename().substring(0, 10) throws a NullPointerException or StringIndexOutOfBoundsException, causing the importer to skip archiving fee-related stream files and leaving the fee schedule in an inconsistent state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getStreamPath()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the dataPath property (hiero.mirror.importer.dataPath) to point to a world-writable directory, so that the lazy-initialized streamPath resolves to an attacker-controlled location where fee-manipulated stream files can be placed for ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getStreamPath()] Can an unprivileged external user set dataPath to a relative path containing '..' segments (e.g., '../../attacker/data') so that streamPath resolves outside the application root, enabling placement of fee-manipulated stream files in the resolved path?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getInitialAddressBook()] Can an unprivileged external user set the initialAddressBook property to a path pointing to a crafted address book file that contains attacker-controlled node public keys, causing the importer to accept signatures from attacker nodes and validate fee-manipulated stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getNodePublicKey()] Can an unprivileged external user set the nodePublicKey property to a public key they control, causing AddressBookServiceImpl to override all node public keys in the address book with the attacker's key, allowing the attacker to sign and validate fee-manipulated stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getNodePublicKey()] Can an unprivileged external user set nodePublicKey to an empty string or whitespace value that bypasses the null check in AddressBookServiceImpl (StringUtils.isNotBlank), causing the public key override to be silently skipped and leaving the importer in an inconsistent key state that could affect fee-related signature validation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getStartDate()] Can an unprivileged external user set startDate to a past timestamp that predates the current fee schedule, causing the importer to re-ingest historical stream files with outdated fee schedules and overwrite current fee data in the database with stale values?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getStartDate()] Can an unprivileged external user set startDate to a future timestamp beyond the current endDate, triggering an InvalidConfigurationException in DateRangeCalculator that halts the importer and prevents legitimate fee schedule updates from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getEndDate()] Can an unprivileged external user set endDate to a timestamp in the past so that the importer immediately disables polling after processing the first file, preventing any future fee schedule updates from being ingested and leaving the fee schedule permanently stale?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getEndDate()] Can an unprivileged external user set endDate to Instant.EPOCH (or near-zero) so that the DateRangeFilter rejects all stream files, causing the importer to process no transactions and leaving the fee schedule in its initial (potentially incorrect) state indefinitely?
